segments.caption: The video opens with a man with a beard, glasses, and visible tattoos on his arms, wearing a light green striped shirt, standing inside a moving train, looking out the window at a passing industrial landscape with shipping containers. The scene transitions to a forward-facing view from the front of the train, showing tracks stretching into a desert-like landscape under a bright blue sky. The man then appears outside at a train station with a covered platform, wooden beams, and potted purple flowers, speaking directly to the camera while gesturing. He walks along the platform, continuing to speak, as the camera follows him. On-screen graphics appear, including a news headline from CBS News about Amtrak suspending service due to storm damage, and a map graphic showing a route from Los Angeles to New York City. The man holds up a small white object, possibly a ticket or card, before the video transitions to a title card reading 'Vox Design, explained' with a yellow circle and pink triangle. The final shot shows the man entering a building with arched doorways and tiled floors, labeled 'Los Angeles,' suggesting the location of the station.

segments.caption: The video opens with a man with a beard, glasses, and colorful tattoos on both arms, wearing a light green striped short-sleeve shirt, standing on a train station platform in Los Angeles, speaking directly to the camera. The scene transitions to the interior of a moving train, showing passengers seated and looking out large windows as the train travels through a desert landscape. The man reappears inside the train, now seated, continuing his narration. A map of the United States appears, illustrating a route from Los Angeles to New York City with a blue line marked by black pins, emphasizing the extensive rail network. The video then cuts to a close-up of a tray table displaying a map of the U.S. rail system, with text overlay stating '1916, 254,000 MILES FREIGHT AND PASSENGER RAIL.' The man holds up a red sign with the Pennsylvania Railroad logo, labeled 'Pennsy,' followed by a vintage illustration of a steam locomotive and a historical sketch of passengers in a train car. The segment concludes with the man speaking again, now in a different train car with a more modern interior, as the video transitions to a title card reading 'Vox Design, explained.'

discovered_new_entities.Vintage train illustration: A colorful illustration of a steam locomotive pulling passenger cars, labeled 'Broadway Limited, Pennsylvania Railroad', with smoke billowing from the engine.
discovered_new_entities.Historical train sketch: A black-and-white sketch depicting 19th-century passengers in formal attire seated inside a train car, with a conductor standing nearby.

segments.caption: The video opens with a historical black-and-white photograph of 19th-century passengers at a train station, setting a nostalgic tone. It transitions to a hand holding a sepia-toned image of Penn Station in 1911, showcasing its grand, cathedral-like interior with towering columns and ornate arches. The scene shifts to a modern train interior, where a man with a beard, glasses, and tattoos speaks directly to the camera, seated among blue seats with a window view of passing scenery. The video then overlays a vintage sketch of a luxurious train dining car with elegantly dressed passengers, followed by a historical menu from the Pennsylvania Railroad dated Tuesday, November 13, 1917, highlighting a meat-free Tuesday and listing items like oysters, scallops, and duck. This is contrasted with a modern Amtrak flexible dining menu, showing contemporary options like chicken ala rosa and baked ziti. The man is later seen eating a meal in the train’s dining car, with a server attending to other passengers. The video then shows the interior of a modern observation car with large windows and curved ceilings, where passengers relax and look out at the landscape. The final shot is an animated map of Southern California, highlighting the train’s route from Los Angeles through San Bernardino and into the desert, marked with a yellow triangle indicating a specific location.

segments.caption: The video opens with an animated route map of Southern California, showing a red dotted line tracing a train’s path from Los Angeles through San Bernardino into the desert, marked with a yellow triangle. The scene transitions to a dark, nighttime view from a moving train, with distant city lights and power lines visible through the window. The camera then cuts to a close-up of a man with a beard, glasses, and colorful tattoos on both arms, wearing a light green striped short-sleeve shirt, speaking directly to the camera inside a dimly lit train car. He appears to be vlogging, with warm overhead lighting illuminating his face. The camera pans to show the train’s interior: rows of blue seats, overhead compartments, and a dimly lit corridor with a sign indicating 'Restrooms' and 'Emergency Exit'. The man is later seen brushing his teeth in a narrow train bathroom, his tattooed arm visible. He then reappears, smiling at the camera, before the scene shifts to him sleeping in a reclined seat, wearing headphones and a sleep mask, with his legs propped up on the window ledge. A time stamp of '3:00 AM' appears as he sleeps. The video ends with him waking up, eating a sandwich in the dining car, with daylight now visible through the window, indicating the passage of time during the journey.

segments.caption: The video opens with a man with a beard, glasses, and colorful tattoos on both arms, wearing a light green striped short-sleeve shirt, sipping from a coffee cup inside a modern train car while speaking directly to the camera. He then walks through the train’s corridor, passing a hand sanitizer dispenser and a 'CAUTION! WATCH YOUR STEP' sign, before entering a luxurious observation car with plush, dark blue velvet seats and ornate ceiling lights. The scene shifts to him seated in a standard blue seat, continuing to speak to the camera as the train moves through greenery and later a desert landscape. The video then transitions to historical black-and-white images: portraits of 'Cornelius Vanderbilt' and 'Jay Gould' labeled as 'Robber barons', followed by a group portrait of 19th-century industrialists. A detailed vintage map of the U.S. rail network is shown, zooming across the Midwest and South. The narrative includes a historical illustration of a crowded train station with a 'SQUARE MEALS, LODGINGS' sign, and a political cartoon depicting Vanderbilt and Gould riding trains labeled 'HUDSON RIVER R.R.', 'N.Y. CENTRAL R.R.', and 'ERIE R.R.', with Vanderbilt commanding, 'NOW THEN JIM—LET EM RIP BUT DONT STOP TO WATER'. The segment concludes with the man back in the modern train, speaking to the camera as the landscape outside shifts from green to arid.

segments.caption: The video opens with a man with a beard, glasses, and a dark blue collared shirt seated inside a modern train car, speaking directly to the camera. The train is in motion, as evidenced by the blurred greenery visible through the window beside him. The interior features blue seats with a geometric pattern, overhead compartments, and a control panel with a speaker and lights. The man gestures as he speaks, and the scene cuts to a historical document titled 'Interstate Commerce Act of 1887,' displayed with a grid overlay and text highlighting its significance. The video returns to the man, who continues his narration. A quote from historian Robert Selph Henry appears on screen: 'The problem, to some minds at least, was how to keep railroads from making too much money.' This is followed by archival black-and-white footage of a train moving through a wooded area, then a shot of early 20th-century automobiles parked outside a brick building. The scene transitions to a pie chart titled 'Passenger Travel in the US,' sourced from the Interstate Commerce Commission 1956 and University of Iowa 1989, showing railroads accounted for 4% of passenger travel in 1956 and 98% in 1916. The video concludes with the man back in the train, continuing his explanation as the scenery outside shifts from green to darker, possibly evening light.

active_entities.Man with beard and tattoos: A man with a beard, glasses, and a dark blue collared shirt, seated inside a modern train car, speaking directly to the camera with expressive gestures. He appears engaged and animated as he explains historical context, with the train moving through changing landscapes visible through the window beside him.
active_entities.Train interior: The interior of a moving train, featuring blue seats with a geometric pattern, overhead compartments, and large windows showing passing greenery that transitions to darker, possibly evening light. The environment includes a control panel with a speaker and lights, and the train’s motion is evident from the blurred scenery outside.

discovered_new_entities.Interstate Commerce Act of 1887 document: A historical document displayed with a grid overlay, titled 'Interstate Commerce Act of 1887,' showing text from the Forty-Ninth Congress of the United States of America, with a date stamp of February 5, 1887. The document is presented as a key historical reference in the narrative.
discovered_new_entities.Quote graphic: A white background with a quote in black text: 'The problem, to some minds at least, was how to keep railroads from making too much money,' attributed to Robert Selph Henry, Historian, with a small yellow triangle icon next to his name.
discovered_new_entities.Archival train footage: Black-and-white footage showing a train moving through a wooded area, viewed from the front, with smoke and trees passing by, evoking early 20th-century rail travel.
discovered_new_entities.Archival automobile footage: Black-and-white footage of early 20th-century automobiles parked outside a brick building with large windows, with people in period attire standing nearby, illustrating the rise of personal transportation.
discovered_new_entities.Passenger Travel in the US pie chart: A dynamic pie chart with a grid background, titled 'Passenger Travel in the US,' sourced from the Interstate Commerce Commission 1956 and University of Iowa 1989. It visually compares railroad passenger share: 4% in 1956 (pink slice) and 98% in 1916 (pink slice), with the rest in blue, highlighting the dramatic decline in rail dominance over time.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man with a beard, glasses, and a dark blue collared shirt, seated inside a modern train car, speaking directly to the camera. He appears animated and engaged, gesturing slightly as he explains historical context. The train interior features blue seats with a geometric pattern, overhead compartments, and large windows showing passing greenery that transitions to darker, possibly evening light. The environment includes a control panel with a speaker and lights, and the train’s motion is evident from the blurred scenery outside. The scene briefly cuts to an exterior shot of a freight train moving across a concrete overpass, carrying colorful shipping containers, with dry, scrubby vegetation below. This is followed by black-and-white archival footage of a steam locomotive moving through a wooded area, evoking early 20th-century rail travel. The video then returns to the man speaking, maintaining the same setting and demeanor. A graphic of the Amtrak logo appears, with blue lines radiating outward to major U.S. cities like Seattle, Chicago, New York, and San Diego, emphasizing the national rail network. The man continues speaking, maintaining eye contact with the camera, as the train continues its journey through changing landscapes.

discovered_new_entities.Freight train overpass: A modern freight train carrying colorful shipping containers moving across a concrete overpass, with dry, scrubby vegetation and utility poles visible below, indicating an industrial or semi-rural setting.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man with a beard, glasses, and a dark blue collared shirt, seated inside a modern train car, speaking directly to the camera. The train is in motion, as evidenced by the blurred greenery visible through the window beside him. He appears engaged and animated, explaining historical context related to rail transportation. The scene cuts to a close-up of a legal document titled 'PUBLIC LAW 91-518—OCT. 30, 1970,' specifically 'TITLE III—CREATION OF A RAIL PASSENGER CORPORATION,' with key phrases like 'for profit corporation' and 'innovative operating and marketing concepts' highlighted in yellow. The video returns to the man, who continues his narration, then cuts again to the same document, now highlighting 'the establishment of improved reservations systems and advertising.' A graphic appears showing 'AMTRAK $2.3 billion' with a source citation. This is followed by a bar chart comparing 'US Highway budget vs. Amtrak budget,' showing 'FEDERAL HIGHWAYS' at $64.3 billion and 'AMTRAK' at $2.3 billion, with sources cited. The man reappears, continuing his explanation with expressive gestures, as the train continues moving through changing landscapes outside the window.

discovered_new_entities.Public Law 91-518 document: A historical legal document titled 'PUBLIC LAW 91-518—OCT. 30, 1970,' specifically 'TITLE III—CREATION OF A RAIL PASSENGER CORPORATION,' with key phrases such as 'for profit corporation' and 'innovative operating and marketing concepts' highlighted in yellow. The document is shown in close-up, with text clearly legible and annotations emphasizing specific sections.
discovered_new_entities.AMTRAK budget graphic: A clean, minimalist graphic displaying 'AMTRAK $2.3 billion' in bold black text with a teal square icon, sourced from the 'Amtrak 2022 Consolidated Financial Statement.' The design is simple and grid-based, emphasizing the financial figure.
discovered_new_entities.US Highway budget vs. Amtrak budget char: A horizontal bar chart comparing 'FEDERAL HIGHWAYS' at $64.3 billion (in pink) and 'AMTRAK' at $2.3 billion (in teal), with the title 'US Highway budget vs. Amtrak budget.' The chart includes source citations from the 'US Department of Transportation 2023 Budget Highlights' and 'Amtrak 2022 Consolidated Financial Statement,' presented on a white grid background.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Man with beard and tattoos seated inside a modern train car, speaking directly to the camera. He wears a dark blue collared shirt and glasses, and the train’s interior features blue patterned seats and large windows showing passing greenery. The scene transitions to a graphic comparing the US Highway budget ($64.3 billion) to Amtrak’s budget ($2.3 billion), with a yellow circle highlighting Amtrak’s smaller allocation. The video then cuts to an exterior shot of a train moving through Chicago, with the city skyline visible in the background and a yellow triangle marker labeling the location. Next, the camera shows a train station platform with passengers boarding, followed by the man walking through a grand train station hall with high ceilings, columns, and a large skylight. He is now wearing a light-colored shirt and continues speaking to the camera. The scene shifts to an outdoor urban setting with tall buildings, where the man, now in a black t-shirt with a city skyline graphic, stands in front of a large digital billboard displaying a close-up of a face. He speaks to the camera again, then the video shows a wide-angle shot of the Cloud Gate sculpture (The Bean) in Chicago’s Millennium Park, with people walking around it. The man reappears in front of the billboard, continuing his narration. The lighting throughout transitions from natural daylight inside the train to bright, sunlit urban environments, emphasizing movement and location changes.

The chart includes source citations from the 'US Department of Transportation 2023 Budget Highlights' and 'Amtrak 2022 Consolidated Financial Statement,' presented on a white grid background.
active_entities.Train station platform: An outdoor train station with a covered wooden-beamed roof, concrete pillars, benches, potted purple flowers, and passengers boarding a train. The platform is bustling with activity, and the train’s side is visible with its red and silver livery.

discovered_new_entities.Chicago: An exterior shot of a train moving through Chicago, with the city skyline visible in the background and a yellow triangle marker labeling the location. The scene includes multiple train tracks and industrial buildings, with the train’s red and silver livery clearly visible.
discovered_new_entities.Grand train station hall: A spacious, ornate train station hall with high ceilings, large columns, a glass skylight, and marble floors. The hall features a 'TO ALL TRAINS' sign, benches, and passengers walking or sitting with luggage. The architecture is classical, with detailed stonework and a large American flag displayed.
discovered_new_entities.Outdoor urban setting: A bright, sunlit urban environment with tall buildings, green trees, and a large digital billboard displaying a close-up of a face. The man stands in front of the billboard, speaking to the camera, with people walking in the background.
discovered_new_entities.Cloud Gate sculpture (The Bean): A large, reflective, bean-shaped sculpture in Chicago’s Millennium Park, with its polished stainless steel surface mirroring the surrounding buildings and sky. People are seen walking around and interacting with the sculpture, and the camera captures a wide-angle, fisheye view of the scene.

segments.caption: The video opens with a man with a beard, glasses, and a dark blue collared shirt standing in a bright, sunlit urban setting, speaking directly to the camera in front of a large digital billboard displaying a close-up of a face. The scene transitions to a wide shot of the Cloud Gate sculpture (The Bean) in Chicago’s Millennium Park, reflecting the city skyline and bustling with people. The man is then shown inside a train, unpacking his luggage in a sleeper car with blue seats and a red suitcase, wearing a black t-shirt with a city skyline graphic. He speaks to the camera while seated, then demonstrates the train’s compact bathroom, pulling out a folding sink and brushing his teeth. He later appears wearing a black sleep mask, still speaking to the camera in the dimly lit sleeper car. The video cuts to a nighttime view from the train window, showing an industrial area with glowing lights and smokestacks, followed by a daytime view of green hills and trees passing by. A map graphic overlays the scene, showing a train route from Chicago to Los Angeles with a yellow triangle marker. The man is then seen lying in his sleeper berth, covered with a blue blanket, before sitting up and adjusting his position. The final shot displays a title card from the Kleinman Center for Energy Policy titled 'Traveling Across the United States the Old-Fashioned Way,' with text describing the author’s 3,000-mile Amtrak journey across three iconic train lines.

active_entities.Man with beard and tattoos: A man with a beard, glasses, and a dark blue collared shirt, seated inside a modern train car, speaking directly to the camera with expressive gestures. He later appears in a light-colored shirt inside a grand train station and then in a black t-shirt with a city skyline graphic in an outdoor urban setting, continuing to speak to the camera.

active_entities.Outdoor urban setting: A bright, sunlit urban environment with tall buildings, green trees, and a large digital billboard displaying a close-up of a face. The man stands in front of the billboard, speaking to the camera, with people walking in the background.

active_entities.Vintage train illustration: A colorful illustration of a steam locomotive pulling passenger cars, labeled 'Broadway Limited, Pennsylvania Railroad', with smoke billowing from the engine, not visible in this segment but contextually relevant.
active_entities.Historical train sketch: A black-and-white sketch depicting 19th-century passengers in formal attire seated inside a train car, with a conductor standing nearby, overlaid with a modern train interior.
active_entities.Penn Station 1911 photograph: A sepia-toned photograph of Penn Station's grand interior from 1911, held by a hand, showing towering columns, arched ceilings, and ornate details.
active_entities.Historical menu (1917): A printed menu from the Pennsylvania Railroad dated Tuesday, November 13, 1917, listing dinner items such as oysters, scallops, duck, and vegetables, with a notice that no meat is served on Tuesdays.
active_entities.Modern Amtrak menu: A printed flexible dining menu from Amtrak, listing contemporary options like chicken ala rosa, baked ziti, and desserts, with a QR code and Amtrak branding.
active_entities.Observation car interior: A modern train car with large, curved windows, a high ceiling, and rows of blue seats, where passengers sit and look out at the passing landscape.
active_entities.Animated route map (Southern California): A gray map of Southern California with a red dotted line tracing a train route from Los Angeles through San Bernardino into the desert, marked with a yellow triangle.
active_entities.Cornelius Vanderbilt portrait: A black-and-white historical portrait of a man with a beard and formal 19th-century attire, labeled 'Cornelius Vanderbilt' with the text 'Robber barons' overlaid.
active_entities.Jay Gould portrait: A black-and-white historical portrait of a man with a full beard and formal 19th-century attire, labeled 'Jay Gould'.
active_entities.Group portrait of industrialists: A black-and-white group photograph of several 19th-century men in formal suits, seated and standing in an ornate room, with one man in a top hat prominently featured.
active_entities.Vintage U.S. rail map: An old, detailed map of the United States showing a dense network of rail lines, with cities like Chicago, St. Louis, and New Orleans labeled, and zooming across the Midwest and South.
active_entities.Historical train station illustration: A black-and-white illustration of a crowded 19th-century train station with people, tents, and signs, including one reading 'SQUARE MEALS, LODGINGS'.
active_entities.Political cartoon of train barons: A black-and-white political cartoon showing two men riding steam locomotives labeled 'HUDSON RIVER R.R.', 'N.Y. CENTRAL R.R.', and 'ERIE R.R.', with one man (Vanderbilt) commanding the other (Gould) to 'LET EM RIP BUT DONT STOP TO WATER'.
active_entities.Interstate Commerce Act of 1887 document: A historical document displayed with a grid overlay, titled 'Interstate Commerce Act of 1887,' showing text from the Forty-Ninth Congress of the United States of America, with a date stamp of February 5, 1887. The document is presented as a key historical reference in the narrative.
active_entities.Quote graphic: A white background with a quote in black text: 'The problem, to some minds at least, was how to keep railroads from making too much money,' attributed to Robert Selph Henry, Historian, with a small yellow triangle icon next to his name.
active_entities.Archival train footage: Black-and-white footage showing a train moving through a wooded area, viewed from the front, with smoke and trees passing by, evoking early 20th-century rail travel.
active_entities.Archival automobile footage: Black-and-white footage of early 20th-century automobiles parked outside a brick building with large windows, with people in period attire standing nearby, illustrating the rise of personal transportation.
active_entities.Passenger Travel in the US pie chart: A dynamic pie chart with a grid background, titled 'Passenger Travel in the US,' sourced from the Interstate Commerce Commission 1956 and University of Iowa 1989. It visually compares railroad passenger share: 4% in 1956 (pink slice) and 98% in 1916 (pink slice), with the rest in blue, highlighting the dramatic decline in rail dominance over time.
active_entities.Amtrak logo graphic: A blue, starburst-style graphic with the word 'Amtrak' at the center, radiating lines to major U.S. cities including Seattle, Chicago, New York, San Diego, and Washington, displayed against a black background.
active_entities.Freight train overpass: A modern freight train carrying colorful shipping containers moving across a concrete overpass, with dry, scrubby vegetation and utility poles visible below, indicating an industrial or semi-rural setting.
active_entities.Public Law 91-518 document: A historical legal document titled 'PUBLIC LAW 91-518—OCT. 30, 1970,' specifically 'TITLE III—CREATION OF A RAIL PASSENGER CORPORATION,' with key phrases such as 'for profit corporation' and 'innovative operating and marketing concepts' highlighted in yellow. The document is shown in close-up, with text clearly legible and annotations emphasizing specific sections.
active_entities.AMTRAK budget graphic: A clean, minimalist graphic displaying 'AMTRAK $2.3 billion' in bold black text with a teal square icon, sourced from the 'Amtrak 2022 Consolidated Financial Statement.' The design is simple and grid-based, emphasizing the financial figure.
active_entities.US Highway budget vs. Amtrak budget char: A horizontal bar chart comparing 'FEDERAL HIGHWAYS' at $64.3 billion (in pink) and 'AMTRAK' at $2.3 billion (in teal), with the title 'US Highway budget vs. Amtrak budget.' The chart includes source citations from the 'US Department of Transportation 2023 Budget Highlights' and 'Amtrak 2022 Consolidated Financial Statement,' presented on a white grid background.
active_entities.Chicago: An exterior shot of a train moving through Chicago, with the city skyline visible in the background and a yellow triangle marker labeling the location. The scene includes multiple train tracks and industrial buildings, with the train’s red and silver livery clearly visible.
active_entities.Grand train station hall: A spacious, ornate train station hall with high ceilings, large columns, a glass skylight, and marble floors. The hall features a 'TO ALL TRAINS' sign, benches, and passengers walking or sitting with luggage. The architecture is classical, with detailed stonework and a large American flag displayed.

discovered_new_entities.Title card (Kleinman Center for Energy P: A white background with the title 'Traveling Across the United States the Old-Fashioned Way' in bold blue text, authored by Andrés S. Svennazi, dated June 16, 2022, with a subtitle explaining the importance of passenger rail in combating climate change. The text describes the author’s 3,000-mile Amtrak journey across three iconic train lines.

segments.caption: The video opens with a title card from the Kleinman Center for Energy Policy, introducing a piece on traveling across the United States by train, emphasizing its environmental and safety benefits. This transitions into a series of Vox graphics highlighting that long-distance trains produce 37% less emissions than direct flights and that travelers are 17 times more likely to die in a car than on a train for the same distance. The scene then shifts to a man with a beard, glasses, and a dark blue collared shirt seated inside a modern train car, speaking directly to the camera. The video cuts to a first-person perspective of passengers boarding a train, followed by a shot from inside a car driving in rainy weather, then back to the man now wearing headphones and a light-colored shirt, looking out the train window. He is later seen walking through the train’s corridor, then relaxing in a sleeper car with a black t-shirt featuring a city skyline graphic, arms behind his head. A bar chart comparing the US Highway budget ($64.3 billion) to Amtrak’s budget ($2.3 billion) is displayed, sourced from official documents. The video then shows a split-screen: on the left, a hand holding a phone with a map of the Northeastern US rail route from New York to Boston, and on the right, the man walking through a train station platform. The final shot shows the man entering the Moynihan Train Hall in New York City, with the location labeled on screen.

active_entities.Man with beard and tattoos: A man with a beard, glasses, and a dark blue collared shirt, seated inside a modern train car, speaking directly to the camera with expressive gestures. He later appears in a light-colored shirt inside a grand train station and then in a black t-shirt with a city skyline graphic in an outdoor urban setting, continuing to speak to the camera.
active_entities.Train interior: The interior of a moving train, featuring blue seats with a geometric pattern, overhead compartments, and large windows showing passing greenery that transitions to darker, possibly evening light. The environment includes a control panel with a speaker and lights, and the train’s motion is evident from the blurred scenery outside.
active_entities.Train station platform: An outdoor train station with a covered wooden-beamed roof, concrete pillars, benches, potted purple flowers, and passengers boarding a train. The platform is bustling with activity, and the train’s side is visible with its red and silver livery.
active_entities.US Highway budget vs. Amtrak budget char: A horizontal bar chart comparing 'FEDERAL HIGHWAYS' at $64.3 billion (in pink) and 'AMTRAK' at $2.3 billion (in teal), with the title 'US Highway budget vs. Amtrak budget.' The chart includes source citations from the 'US Department of Transportation 2023 Budget Highlights' and 'Amtrak 2022 Consolidated Financial Statement,' presented on a white grid background.

active_entities.Title card (Kleinman Center for Energy P: A white background with the title 'Traveling Across the United States the Old-Fashioned Way' in bold blue text, authored by Andrés S. Svennazi, dated June 16, 2022, with a subtitle explaining the importance of passenger rail in combating climate change. The text describes the author’s 3,000-mile Amtrak journey across three iconic train lines.
active_entities.News headline graphic: A text overlay from CBS News reading 'Amtrak suspends train service in New York due to storm damage', with a red highlight on 'suspends train service', not visible in this segment.
active_entities.Grand train station hall: A spacious, ornate train station hall with high ceilings, large columns, a glass skylight, and marble floors. The hall features a 'TO ALL TRAINS' sign, benches, and passengers walking or sitting with luggage. The architecture is classical, with detailed stonework and a large American flag displayed.

discovered_new_entities.New York City: A text overlay labeling the location as 'New York City' as the man enters the Moynihan Train Hall, with the station’s modern, clean interior visible, including glass doors, metal railings, and signage.

segments.caption: The video opens with a wide-angle, fisheye shot of a bustling New York City street, where pedestrians cross a zebra-striped crosswalk amidst tall buildings and traffic, establishing an urban setting. The scene transitions to a man with a beard, glasses, and a dark blue collared shirt, pulling a red suitcase through the city streets, navigating past a double-decker bus and other pedestrians. He enters a dimly lit hotel room, drops his suitcase, and dramatically leaps onto the bed, conveying a sense of arrival and relief. After a moment, he stands up, turns to the camera, and begins speaking directly to the viewer, his expression animated and engaging. On-screen text appears, reading '3,224 MILES,' emphasizing the distance of his journey. The scene then cuts to him inside a moving train, now wearing a light-colored striped shirt, holding a camera up to his face, his tattooed arms visible as he photographs the passing scenery. The video shifts to black-and-white footage from the train window, showing a fence, trees, and tracks blurring past, followed by a color shot of a train crossing a bridge over a river, surrounded by lush greenery. The final frames show the train tracks from a moving perspective, with the landscape rushing by, reinforcing the theme of travel and motion.

active_entities.Man with beard and tattoos: A man with a beard, glasses, and a dark blue collared shirt, initially seen pulling a red suitcase through New York City streets, then entering a hotel room, leaping onto a bed, and speaking directly to the camera. Later, he appears in a light-colored striped shirt inside a moving train, holding a camera with tattooed arms visible, capturing the scenery.
active_entities.New York City: A text overlay labeling the location as 'New York City' appears over a wide-angle shot of a busy street with pedestrians crossing a zebra-striped crosswalk, tall buildings, and traffic, establishing the urban environment.
active_entities.Train interior: The interior of a moving train, featuring large windows through which the passing landscape is visible. The man is seen holding a camera, his tattooed arms prominent, as he photographs the scenery.

discovered_new_entities.Hotel room: A dimly lit, modern hotel room with a large bed, white linens, a desk with bottles, a mounted TV, and dark curtains. The man enters, drops his suitcase, leaps onto the bed, and then stands to speak to the camera.
discovered_new_entities.Train tracks: Multiple shots of train tracks from a moving perspective, including black-and-white footage showing tracks and a fence, and a color shot of tracks alongside a river with a bridge in the background, emphasizing the journey's motion and environment.
discovered_new_entities.Red suitcase: A bright red rolling suitcase with an extended handle, pulled by the man through the streets of New York City and later placed in the hotel room.
discovered_new_entities.On-screen text '3,224 MILES': White text appearing on screen during the man’s monologue in the hotel room, emphasizing the distance of his journey, with a slight animation as it types out.
discovered_new_entities.Camera: A black camera with a strap, held by the man in the train, used to photograph the passing scenery, with his tattooed arms visible as he holds it up to his face.

segments.caption: The video opens with a blurred, fast-moving shot of train tracks, suggesting motion, before transitioning to a clear, static view of a detailed Amtrak network map of the United States displayed on a tray table. The map highlights various routes in red and green lines across the country, with major cities labeled. The camera then cuts to an exterior shot of an Amtrak train, focusing on its silver body with red and blue stripes and the Amtrak logo. Next, the scene shifts to a brick train station building with a large blue Amtrak sign and a modern glass facade. The video then cuts to a man with a beard, glasses, and a dark blue collared shirt, standing in a dimly lit hotel room, speaking directly to the camera. He appears to be narrating or explaining something, with a bed and modern decor visible behind him. This is followed by a text graphic quoting Graham Claytor, former CEO of Amtrak: 'When I came to Amtrak I was convinced I could save it, but finally came to the conclusion that only the American public can save it when they say they want it.' The quote is displayed on a grid background with 'Amtrak' highlighted in blue and 'when they say they want it' highlighted in yellow. The man reappears in the hotel room, continuing his narration. The video then transitions to the man seated inside a moving train, reclined in a blue seat, with his eyes closed and a calm expression, suggesting he is resting or sleeping. On-screen credits roll on the left side, listing production roles and sources, including Amtrak, the Department of Transportation, and various music tracks. The final shot shows a view from the train window, with trees and clouds passing by rapidly, indicating the train’s speed, as the credits continue to roll.

active_entities.Tray table map: A detailed map of the United States rail system displayed on a white tray table, showing Amtrak routes in red and green lines, with major cities labeled and a legend at the bottom indicating 'Amtrak Train Routes' and 'Service Suspended'.

active_entities.Train interior: The interior of a moving train, featuring a blue seat with a headrest, a small overhead control panel, and the man reclined with his eyes closed, suggesting rest or sleep.

discovered_new_entities.Amtrak train exterior: The side of a silver Amtrak train with red and blue stripes and the Amtrak logo prominently displayed, captured in a close-up shot.
discovered_new_entities.Train station building: A brick train station building with a large blue Amtrak sign and a modern glass facade, visible in the background behind the train.
discovered_new_entities.Credits overlay: A black vertical bar on the left side of the screen listing production credits, sources, images, footage, thanks, and music, including names like Dean Peterson, Amtrak, Getty, and music tracks such as 'Big and Whimsical' and 'Cowboys All Ride Away'.
discovered_new_entities.Passing landscape from train window: A view from a moving train window showing a bright blue sky with white clouds and green trees passing by rapidly, indicating motion and speed.
